title: Hosted and Self-Hosted Runners, Isolation, Capacity, and Compatibility · Pipeline Repair Bay
summary: A release responder inherits a plausible but defective runner isolation and capacity policy; preserve run evidence, isolate the first causal fault in hosted and self-hosted runners, isolation, capacity, and compatibility, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in runner isolation and capacity policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggi…
objectives: Pin or record runner OS, architecture, installed software, action runtime, image release, and migration assumptions instead of trusting latest as immutable.
Threat-model persistence, workspace residue, credentials, network reachability, daemon sockets, labels, groups, and repository eligibility for self-hosted runners.
Design single-job ephemeral runner registration, identity, network segmentation, secret delivery, teardown, log retention, and orphan cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Ci Hosted Runner Image Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug public pull request reaches a persistent self-hosted runner with Docker authority and cloud credentials while 48 jobs queue. Bound eligibility, ephemerality, compatibility, capacity, evidence, and destruction. Competency probe: Pin or record runner OS, architecture, installed software, action runtime, image release, and migration assumptions instead of trusting latest as immutable.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-runner-compatibility-action-node24-1pyx3i". Tailor its runner-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24 into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24, keep Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24 connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-runner-compatibility-action-node24-1pyx3i
  - competency: ci-runner-compatibility-action-node24
    artifact: runner-policy.yaml
    decision: runner compatibility action node24
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: make the service container start before it is ready
    retained-proof: queue duration critical path failure class recovery and cost evidence
    case-key: 1pyx3i
    change: |
      runner-image: ubuntu-24.04-reviewed
      action-runtime: node24
      minimum-runner: 2.327.1-or-later
      untrusted-work: github-hosted-ephemeral
      self-hosted: trusted-release-only
      network: segmented
      cleanup: single-job-destroy
      capacity: queue-budget-and-max-parallel
    verify: inspect runner version image release labels groups network authority queue duration and teardown evidence
    failure: Reject latest as immutable, reused public-PR runners, workspace cleanup as host destruction, or unbounded parallelism.
    repair: Record compatibility, isolate untrusted work, destroy ephemeral capacity, segment authority, and measure queue and cost.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
